This evening, really the title for the series, if you want a serious title, it's from the first statement, which we'll be studying this evening. In the Lord put I my trust. And I'm gonna title the message this evening, the Declaration of the Psalmist, because what a declaration this is to start the Psalm, where the Psalmist says in the Lord, put I my trust. We're gonna think about this particular Psalm. We don't know the exact circumstances, but it's believed that most likely David is fleeing from Saul. And if you read through the book of first Samuel, you'll read of that whole interaction and the way in which David was running and fleeing from Saul and Saul had made David his enemy continually. This expression zeroes in on a deliberate decision of an individual. I am going to purpose, I'm going to decide, I'm going to make the decision with my will, an act of the will, that I am going to put, I'm going to place my trust, in the case of our study this evening, in the Lord. We find that here, the psalmist is going to put his trust in the Lord. The word trust is an interesting word, it means confidence, but it goes beyond confidence. Here's what the 1828 Noel Webster's Dictionary says for trust, reliance or resting of the mind, on the integrity, veracity, justice, friendship, or other sound principle of another person. Now we know that we're not just speaking of any other person, we see the definition that's given of trust, and no doubt, there's been a time in your life, there's been plenty of times in my life, where someone would say to me, or even I would say to someone else, trust me. Has anyone ever told you that? Trust me, you can trust me on this one. You've heard people say that, and there was a time in our society in which there were many deals that were brokered by just a handshake in understanding the trust that you could place in an individual, and you could trust their word, and now you sign a contract that's 150 pages long, and they still find ways to break it, and the reality is that this evening, when we're talking about this subject of trust, this reliance, this resting of the mind on the integrity, veracity, justice, friendship, or other sound principle of our God, he's trustworthy, he's trustworthy. We find this evening that this declaration of the psalmist, where he says in Psalm 11, in verse number one, in addition to all the other places in which we've read this evening, he says, in the Lord put I my trust. He deliberately put his trust in the Lord, his God. Again, it eliminates passivity. It zeroes in on a deliberate decision of an individual. You and I face things every single day. And the question is, where do I put my trust? Where do I put my trust? Why is it, what is it that led to David, the sweet psalmist of Israel, saying and declaring in the Lord, put I my trust? Well, look back, maybe on the same page for you, a page back for me in Psalm 9, and I want you to notice in verse number 10, Psalm 9 in verse number 10, notice what the Bible says in this verse of Scripture, and they that know thy name will put their trust in thee, for thou, Lord, hast not forsaken them that seek thee. There's great significance here in this verse of Scripture, and it will be our next study on Wednesday evening, as I mentioned a moment ago, where we'll begin studying the names of God, and here the psalmist says, and they that know thy name. Listen, if you know who God is, if you know the names that God has given to us that describe his character and his attributes and who he is, then it says here in this verse that they that know thy name will put their trust in thee. You've been in a situation where you've trusted what someone said because of the character of the individual, maybe somebody spoke up for them and said, no, you can trust them, I know their character. Well, we have a Bible that reveals to us the character and attribute of God, and it says here that they that know thy name will put their trust in thee. I'm afraid today that many of God's people really do not know God in a deep, intimate way, and therefore, they're not putting their trust in God, they're putting their trust in other things, maybe even themselves, because they really don't know, intimately know, who God really is. We find here that in Psalm 11, the psalmist, why would he say this? Well, because he knew the Lord. David knew God. David knew God. David walked with God. Was David perfect? No, in fact, God records for us sin in the life of David, and we see that, but we also see his response and his brokenness and great truth that helps us to be able to relate to an individual that walked with the Lord, that sinned greatly, but he repented and he had forgiveness and restoration, and we see a man that knew the Lord and he declared his trust in the Lord. Not only do we know from Psalm 9 in verse number 10 that they that know, as it says here, thy name will put their trust in thee, but we find that we've been commanded, we've been instructed throughout God's word to trust him. I want you to remember this evening that the Bible tells us repeatedly that the Lord is our helper. The Bible tells us that our God is a very present help in the time of trouble. There are many verses of scripture in fact, that we'll look at next week that provide for us what the results are for those that trust the Lord and how God blesses and the rewards that we'll receive by trusting him. And we find that there are other declarations in which God uses in his word with regard to trusting. It's Job that says, though he slay me, yet I will trust him. And we find here this evening that when we look at that little word put, it's so vital, it's so significant for us this evening because the reality is that when we face a circumstance, when you face a scenario today, when you face a scenario tomorrow, when there's something that comes in your life, when there's something that comes in my life, when there's something that comes in our lives collectively as a church, as a body of believers, are we going to take and have this situation when we look at this word put, again, eliminating the passivity, but zeroing in on the deliberate decision that though all of these things are what the scenarios are and here's what people are saying, but I have purposed, I am determined, I have put my trust in the Lord. It's a decision that we must make. And it's a decision that we make repeatedly. It's a decision that you make on a regular basis throughout the day. You wake up in the morning and say, I'm gonna trust the Lord today, I'm gonna put my trust in the Lord, and then you're gonna face a scenario, you're gonna face a situation in which you're going to be tempted to trust yourself. You're gonna be tempted to trust some other means, some other thing, but we find that in that moment at that crosshair, we must say, no, I put my trust in the Lord. The Psalm in Psalm 11 in verse one says, how say ye to my soul, flee as a bird to your mountain. Have you ever heard anyone say that, use the expression, I wish I could just fly away? And I'm not talking about Southwest and their little catch, you know, marketing tag. I'm just talking about people that say, I wish I could just fly away. I wish I could just get out. What do they mean by that? I wish I could get out of the situation. And here there's some people that are saying to David, he says, he asked the question, how say ye to my soul, flee as a bird to your mountain. Can I tell you this evening that there are times in which other people, but there are also times in which maybe you talk to yourself and say, I wish I could get away. You ever talk to yourself? Come on, we're not gonna, you know, let's take you to the institution or anything if you're talking to yourself. I'm just, it happens, right? You talk to yourself. Listen, I have declared, I have put my trust in the Lord. So no matter what comes, no matter what happens, no matter what circumstance, no matter the difficulty, the depth of the difficulty, the people and what they're saying, no, I have made this decision. I have made the deliberate decision and I am declaring for others to know that in the Lord put I my trust. And so in this evening, the challenge is really simple for us. As I mentioned by way of introduction, that though the Psalm has just seven verses, that there are many ways in which we'll be challenged as we study these seven verses. And tonight the challenge is this. Can we declare as the psalmist did, in the Lord put I my trust? That I've made that deliberate decision that no matter what comes my way, no matter what the circumstances and no matter what the other people are saying, I have put my trust in the Lord. Why? Because the Lord is trustworthy, because we know his name, because we have this book that he gave us, the Bible that reveals to us his nature and his character and who he is and what he's capable of doing. And I say, no, no, no, I'm not trusting in man, I'm not trusting in princes, I'm not trusting in my sword, I'm not trusting in any of these things. No, I'm trusting in the Lord and I'm gonna put my trust in God. And how important it is for God's people to stand as David did in the midst of difficulty and say in the Lord put I my trust. Let's pray together. Father, we love you tonight. We thank you for loving us. God, I pray that you'd help us tonight. I know that in a room, a crowd of any size, there are difficulties that we face. Lord, sometimes those difficulties can bring discouragement in our lives, can overwhelm us. And God, I pray that tonight you would help us from this half a verse of scripture as we look at Psalm 11 one and then coupled with so many other verses of scripture tonight. Lord, would you help us to be able to declare as the psalmist did in the Lord put I my trust. Understanding the importance of being actively engaged in making that decision. Understanding that you are trustworthy. And though in our own human frailty, we may be tempted to trust in other things. things, including ourselves. Lord, I pray tonight that you would help us to put our trust in thee. We thank you for the privilege of Wednesday evenings, not only being able to study your word, but spend time together as a church family in prayer. And I pray God that you'd bless our time of prayer tonight. I pray that you would help us, Lord, to exalt and magnify you. Lord, help us to seek you. We thank you that you're a God who hears and answers and that you're able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think. We pray that you'd have your will and way, for it's in Christ's name we pray. Amen.
